name: Freestyle Catch
description: High elbow catch technique for efficient freestyle swimming
keyPoints: Hand entry should be fingertips first, then hand
Elbows should be high and wide during catch
Head should be neutral, eyes looking forward
Catch should be smooth and continuous
Pull water straight back, not up or down
commonMistakes: Pulling water straight up instead of back
Stopping pull at hips instead of extending fully

name: Freestyle Pull
description: Underwater pull technique with proper body rotation and arm extension
keyPoints: Pull with high elbow position
Rotate body from side to side during pull
Keep pull long and powerful
Head position neutral, looking down slightly
Pull water straight back with acceleration
Maintain continuous propulsion throughout pull
commonMistakes: Pulling with straight arms instead of high elbows
Looking up instead of down during pull

name: Freestyle Breathing
description: Bilateral breathing pattern for efficient oxygen intake and rhythm
keyPoints: Breathe every 2-3 strokes for beginners
Turn head to side to breathe
Exhale completely underwater
Inhale quickly as head turns back
Maintain consistent breathing rhythm
Time breathing with arm recovery
commonMistakes: Breathing too late in stroke cycle
Lifting head too high out of water

name: Freestyle Kick
description: Flutter kick technique for propulsion and body position maintenance
keyPoints: Kick from hips, not knees
Keep ankles relaxed and flexible
Small, rapid kicks for efficiency
Point toes slightly inward
Maintain steady rhythm with arms
Kick continuously throughout stroke cycle
Keep kicks underwater and close to surface

name: Backstroke Catch
description: Double arm catch technique with proper hand entry and body position
keyPoints: Hands enter water pinky finger first
Arms extend straight overhead during recovery
Head stays back, looking up at ceiling
Hips stay high throughout stroke
Pull with straight arms and proper rotation
Catch water at the deepest point

name: Backstroke Pull
description: Underwater pull technique with proper body rotation and arm movement
keyPoints: Pull with straight arms down the centerline
Rotate body properly from side to side
Keep head still and looking up
Pull water straight back toward feet
Initiate hip drive as arms begin pull
Maintain continuous pull throughout underwater phase

name: Backstroke Breathing
description: Breathing technique for backstroke with proper timing and body position
keyPoints: Breathe in as arms start recovery
Hold breath during pull phase
Exhale as arms enter water
Keep head position stable during breathing
Time breathing with arm recovery phase
Maintain consistent breathing rhythm
commonMistakes: Breathing too early or too late

name: Backstroke Kick
description: Dolphin kick technique for backstroke propulsion and body position
keyPoints: Kick from hips with straight legs
Feet flex downward during kick
Ankles relaxed and toes pointed
Small, rapid kicks for propulsion
Kick continuously throughout stroke
Keep knees relatively straight
Maintain steady rhythm with arms

name: Breaststroke Pull
description: Heart-shaped pull technique with proper timing and coordination
keyPoints: Pull with arms in a heart shape pattern
Hands enter water together and pull back simultaneously
Keep elbows high during pull phase
Head position neutral, looking down
Pull water straight back toward feet
Coordinate breathing with arm movement
commonMistakes: Pulling with straight arms instead of heart shape
Hands entering water at different times
Pulling too wide or too narrow

name: Breaststroke Kick
description: Whip kick technique for breaststroke propulsion and proper timing
keyPoints: Kick with heels toward buttocks
Feet flex and toes pointed outward
Kick in heart shape pattern
Coordinate kick with arm recovery
Keep knees relatively straight
Kick powerfully and quickly
Recover legs together during glide

name: Breaststroke Timing
description: Coordination of pull, kick, and breathing for efficient breaststroke
keyPoints: Pull and kick timing coordination
Breathing at the right moment in stroke cycle
Glide phase after kick for maximum efficiency
Smooth transition between pull and kick
Maintain consistent rhythm throughout stroke

name: Butterfly Pull
description: Double arm pull technique with powerful body undulation
keyPoints: Pull with straight arms wide apart
Enter water with hands facing down/backward
Pull water straight back with power
Coordinate arms simultaneously throughout pull
Body undulation from hips through shoulders
Head position neutral, looking down
commonMistakes: Hands entering water too close together
Pulling too narrow or too wide

name: Butterfly Timing
description: Advanced coordination of two-arm pull with dolphin kick for maximum power
keyPoints: Powerful double arm pull with proper timing
Aggressive dolphin kick with body undulation
Second dolphin kick during arm recovery
Early breathing to maximize underwater time
Full body undulation and rhythm
Explosive timing at start of each stroke
commonMistakes: Poor timing between arms and kick
